---
about:
  template: trestles
  image: images/LongCat.jpg
  image-width: 100%
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/sconte4/LongCat_E1/tree/main
    - icon: globe
      text: Lab website
      href: https://sites.google.com/binghamton.edu/encode/home
    - icon: file-text
      text: Publication
      href: https://sites.google.com/binghamton.edu/encode/home/publications?authuser=0

citation:
  type: article-journal
  title: TBD
  issued: TBD
  author:
    - TBD
    - TBD
    - TBD
  container-title: TBD
  doi: TBD
---

## LongCat

The goal of the LongCat study (E1) is to longitudinally track the neural correlates of face perception in infancy.

Data collection is still ongoing by the ENCoDe research Team at Binghamton University. There are up to three timepoints of data collection with 6-, 9-, and 12-month-old infants.

Data are available by [CC-0](https://creativecommons.org/public-domain/cc0/) license.
